text.title: Jenkins Pipeline Structure
text.summary: Declarative pipeline syntax with essential options, parallel stages, when conditions, and post-build actions.

rule.statement: Default to declarative syntax. Use scripted (node block) only when you need dynamic logic such as detecting changed services at runtime.
rule.rationale: Declarative is readable, maintainable, and supports all built-in features. Scripted requires Groovy expertise and is harder to review.

rule.statement: Every pipeline must include: timeout, timestamps, buildDiscarder, and disableConcurrentBuilds in the options block.
rule.rationale: timeout prevents hung builds consuming agents; buildDiscarder prevents disk exhaustion; disableConcurrentBuilds prevents race conditions on shared environments.

example.code: options { timeout(time: 30, unit: 'MINUTES') timestamps() buildDiscarder(logRotator(numToKeepStr: '10')) disableConcurrentBuilds() ansiColor('xterm') }

rule.statement: Use parallel blocks for independent tasks (unit tests, integration tests, security scans) to reduce total build time.
rule.rationale: Sequential execution of independent tasks wastes time. Parallel execution of three 5-minute stages takes 5 minutes, not 15.

rule.statement: Gate production deployments with an input block requiring named approvers. Use when { branch 'main' } to prevent feature branches from deploying.
rule.rationale: Unnamed approvals allow anyone to click through. Named submitter restriction enforces change control without external tooling.

rule.statement: Always clean the workspace in post { always { cleanWs() } }. Send failure notifications in post { failure { ... } }.
rule.rationale: Stale workspace files cause intermittent failures in subsequent builds. Post-failure notifications alert the team without requiring manual monitoring.

antipattern.description: Hardcoding secrets directly in the pipeline environment block.
antipattern.reason: Secrets in Jenkinsfiles are committed to version control and visible in build logs. Use withCredentials() or the credentials() binding instead.
